question: Which mount type is recommended for storing database data in production?

options.text: Named volumes
Bind mounts
options.explanation: Bind mounts are tied to specific host paths and are recommended for development, not production data

options.text: tmpfs
options.explanation: tmpfs stores data only in RAM — all data is lost when the container stops

options.text: Writing to the container's writable layer
options.explanation: Data in the writable layer is lost when the container is removed. Not suitable for persistent data

options.text: --mount is more explicit and self-documenting
--mount returns an error if the path doesn't exist, while -v silently creates a directory
-v doesn't support named volumes
options.explanation: -v supports both named volumes (-v mydata:/data) and bind mounts (-v /host/path:/container/path)

options.text: --mount is recommended for scripts and CI/CD

question: What is tmpfs mounting used for?

options.text: Storing secrets that shouldn't be written to disk
Temporary files with high I/O speed
Long-term database storage
options.explanation: tmpfs stores data only in RAM. All data disappears when the container stops

options.text: Cache and sessions that don't need to persist across restarts

question: What does the :ro flag do when mounting a volume?

options.text: Makes the mount read-only inside the container
Enables volume data rotation
options.explanation: :ro means read-only — the container cannot write data to this volume

options.text: Creates the volume in root-only mode
Automatically creates a volume backup
options.explanation: :ro simply prevents writes. Backups must be created manually using docker run with tar

question: Why might docker run -v ./src:/app/src not work as expected?

options.text: Docker may interpret the relative path as a volume name instead of a bind mount
The -v flag doesn't support bind mounts
options.explanation: -v supports bind mounts but requires an absolute path. Use $(pwd)/src instead of ./src

options.text: The src directory must be empty
options.explanation: Bind mount works with any directory content. The issue is the path syntax

options.text: You need to pre-create a volume with docker volume create
options.explanation: No volume is needed for bind mount. The issue is that Docker distinguishes volumes from bind mounts by path format
